sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Angle Advisors announces Brunner Manufacturing has been acquired by Howmet Aerospace
feed.summary: Angle Advisors announced that Brunner Manufacturing has been acquired by Howmet Aerospace, becoming part of Howmet Fastening Systems. Brunner, a leading manufacturer of fasteners and high-strength components for agricultural, industrial, and commercial vehicles, will allow Howmet to expand its product portfolio and manufacturing capabilities. Angle Advisors acted as the exclusive investment banking advisor to Brunner in this transaction.

feed.title: Taseko to Release Fourth Quarter and Year End 2025 Results
feed.summary: Taseko Mines Limited (TSX: TKO; NYSE American: TGB; LSE: TKO) announced it will release its fourth quarter and year-end 2025 financial results after market close on Wednesday, February 18, 2026. The company will host a telephone conference call and live webcast on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results, which will include a question and answer session for analysts and investors.

feed.title: TER Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ) $314.05 intraday 13 Feb 2026: AI demand guides outlook
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) experienced a 2.30% intraday decline to $314.05 on February 13, 2026, amid profit-taking after a rally and sector rotation towards AI chip demand. Despite this, the stock shows strong long-term appreciation with its exposure to AI hardware cycles through its Semiconductor Test and Industrial Automation segments. Meyka AI rates TER as a "BUY" with a B+ grade (76.51/100), balancing its high valuation against strong cash generation and low leverage, though its forecasting model projects short-term downside.

feed.title: J&J’s baby powder caused a woman’s fatal cancer, Philly jury finds, awarding her family $250,000
feed.summary: A Philadelphia jury has ordered Johnson & Johnson to pay $250,000 to the family of Gayle Emerson, finding that the company's talc-based baby powder caused her ovarian cancer. Emerson, who died months after filing her complaint in 2019, was part of a nationwide wave of litigation against J&J. The verdict included $50,000 in compensatory damages and $200,000 in punitive damages, despite J&J's defense that the baby powder was not responsible and Emerson had other risk factors.

feed.title: Exclusive: How John Deere Is Navigating the Ag Downturn, Equipment Costs and Regulatory Crosswinds
feed.summary: Deere & Company President Deanna Kovar discusses how the company is managing current challenges in the agricultural equipment market, including an ag downturn, high equipment costs, and regulatory issues. While forecasting a continued decline in the North American ag equipment market for 2026, Deere is adjusting production, reducing parts prices, and exploring alternative fuel technologies like an E-98 ethanol tractor. The company is also addressing concerns around "Right to Repair" and DEF systems, while emphasizing the importance of technology retrofits and data-driven farming decisions.

feed.title: Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat all saw gains after a Consumer Price Index (CPI) report came in lower than anticipated, increasing investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. The softened inflation data, with prices rising 0.2% from December to January against a 0.3% forecast, is leading to increased bets on multiple rate reductions this year, stimulating a market rally. NetApp's shares, in particular, remain somewhat volatile but were positively impacted by the news, though they are still down for the year.

feed.title: Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax, saw significant jumps following a softer-than-anticipated Consumer Price Index (CPI) report. This fueled investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, leading to a rally in affected stocks. The Russell 2000 index, comprising smaller companies, reacted particularly strongly to the positive economic news.

feed.title: How to cut data loss risks when employees leave
feed.summary: Employee departures, whether voluntary or involuntary, significantly increase the risk of data loss for organizations. Malicious insider attacks and even accidental data exfiltration can be costly, with financial gain, espionage, and anger often motivating breaches. Implementing a strong Data Loss Prevention (DLP) program, including data classification, continuous monitoring, access controls, and a thorough offboarding process, is crucial to mitigate these risks and protect company assets.

feed.title: Caterpillar group president Kaiser sells $1.3m in stock
feed.summary: Caterpillar Group President Jason Kaiser sold 1,690 shares of company stock for a total of $1.31 million on February 12, 2026. This transaction occurred while the stock was near its 52-week high and showed a 117% return over the past year. InvestingPro analysis suggests Caterpillar is currently overvalued, despite the company reporting strong Q4 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations.

feed.title: Is Viatris (VTRS) Quietly Repositioning Its Deal-Making Strategy With a New Chief Legal Officer?
feed.summary: Viatris (VTRS) recently appointed Matthew J. Maletta as Chief Legal Officer, effective February 9, 2026, signaling a potential reinforcement of its legal capabilities for complex corporate transactions and regulatory matters. Maletta's extensive pharmaceutical legal experience across generics and branded drugs could influence Viatris's investment narrative and risk profile. This strategic hire comes as Viatris continues to focus on broadening its higher-value cardiovascular portfolio and addressing ongoing regulatory issues.

feed.title: Sunoco LP to Announce Q4 Earnings on February 17
feed.summary: Sunoco LP is scheduled to report its Q4 earnings on February 17 before market open, with expected EPS at $0.07 and revenue at $9.36 billion, a significant year-over-year increase. The company has a history of exceeding earnings and revenue estimates, and analysts have recently upgraded its rating to 'Buy' due to its attractive 7% dividend yield. Despite positive performance history, recent estimate revisions indicate cautious market sentiment.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson ordered to pay $250,000 in talc powder lawsuit
feed.summary: A Pennsylvania state court jury has ordered Johnson & Johnson to pay $250,000 to the family of a woman who alleged their talc-based baby powder caused her ovarian cancer. The lawsuit, filed by Gayle Emerson in 2019, claimed the company failed to warn consumers despite knowing the dangers. The jury awarded $50,000 in compensatory damages and $200,000 in punitive damages to her family.

feed.title: Beloved Restaurant Permanently Closing All Locations
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants, Inc., parent company of Bahama Breeze, announced the permanent closure of all Bahama Breeze locations. Half of the 28 remaining restaurants will close their doors completely, while the other 14 will be converted into other Darden brands. This decision follows a previous closure of 15 Bahama Breeze locations in May 2025, with the company stating that the brand was no longer a strategic priority.

feed.title: Vertex’s CRISPR therapy rebounds in latest earnings
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals saw its shares jump after reporting stronger-than-expected sales for its CRISPR gene-edited therapy, Casgevy, tripling sales from the previous quarter. The company reported $54 million in Casgevy sales for Q4 2025, contributing to its overall $12 billion in revenue for the year, and analysts are optimistic about its future sales potential for 2026. Despite challenges like the lengthy treatment process and chemotherapy side effects, patient adoption increased significantly, and Vertex anticipates smoother operations by 2027, further bolstered by potential FDA approval for younger patients and new experimental drugs.

feed.title: Byron Donalds trades in Bitcoin, Howmet Aerospace, and The Trade Desk stocks
feed.summary: Representative Byron Donalds of Florida's 19th congressional district engaged in several stock market transactions recently, as disclosed in a congressional trade report. These transactions included purchasing Bitcoin, and selling portions of his holdings in Howmet Aerospace Inc. and The Trade Desk, Inc. - Class A, all within the range of $1,001 to $15,000 and conducted through his Moran Wealth IRA.

feed.title: T-Mobile US Inc.: How America’s Un?Carrier Turned Its Network Into a Product Powerhouse
feed.summary: T-Mobile US Inc. has transformed its 5G network, plans, and bundled services into a comprehensive product strategy that challenges competitors like AT&T and Verizon. The company's "Un-Carrier" approach focuses on transparency, value-rich subscriptions, and a strong digital ecosystem to drive growth and customer loyalty. This product-centric strategy, combined with technological advantages and a clear brand identity, has positioned T-Mobile as a leading force in the U.S. wireless market, reflected in its stock performance.

feed.title: Ross Stores Stock: Quiet Discount Giant, Loud Returns – Is The Rally Just Getting Started?
feed.summary: Ross Stores (ROST) has demonstrated strong performance, quietly outperforming the broader market due to resilient consumer demand for bargains. Despite being a brick-and-mortar discounter, the company leverages its off-price model to achieve consistent growth and strong shareholder value, even in an uncertain economy. Recent catalysts include better-than-expected quarterly earnings, disciplined inventory management, and strategic store growth, all supported by a bullish Wall Street consensus.

feed.title: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) Reaches New 52-Week High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) recently hit a new 52-week high, trading as high as $143.94 and closing at $140.91. Analyst ratings are generally positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average price target of $147.69. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.165, payable on April 15th, representing a 3.2% yield.

feed.title: Salesforce Stock Slumps As Employees Push Company To Distance From ICE
feed.summary: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) is experiencing a stock slump and internal turmoil as employees protest against a joke made by CEO Marc Benioff regarding ICE and the company's potential partnership with the immigration agency. Employees are pushing for Salesforce to denounce ICE's actions, refrain from providing technology like Agentforce AI to the agency, and support legislation to regulate ICE. This situation highlights the growing pressure on companies to align their operations with public and employee ethical concerns, especially when involving government agencies like ICE.

feed.title: Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) Price Target Raised to C$209.00
feed.summary: CIBC World Markets has raised its price target for Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) from C$199.00 to C$209.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 9.10% from the stock's current price. Other analysts have also recently adjusted their price objectives for BMO, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of C$189.92 for the company.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) Maintains Dividend and Exceeds Earnings Expectations
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has maintained its quarterly dividend of $1.61 per share and exceeded recent earnings estimates due to improved operating margins. The company targets an EPS of $11.20 and a 100 basis point margin expansion by 2026. While ITW demonstrates strong financial health with robust profitability and a diversified business model, its high debt-to-equity ratio and near historical valuation highs warrant vigilance from investors.

feed.title: Ralph Lauren’s Distribution, Marketing, and Product Investments Support Its Margins and Brand Value
feed.summary: Morningstar analyst David Swartz reports on Ralph Lauren's solid footing due to past restructuring efforts, which have resulted in improved gross margins and positioned the brand for low-single-digit sales growth. The company's strategy includes increasing direct-to-consumer sales to 75% by fiscal 2035 and maintaining mid-single-digit advertising support, reducing reliance on physical retail and enhancing control over pricing and positioning. Despite shares being considered overvalued, the company's brand value is affirmed by standout earnings.

feed.title: Did HP just issue stark dividend darning for investors?
feed.summary: HP recently announced a dividend hike, marking 10 consecutive years of increases, which might appear positive initially. However, the company is facing significant headwinds, including rising memory costs impacting earnings and a slower-than-expected adoption of AI-powered PCs and Windows 11 upgrades, especially among price-sensitive customers. While HP's free cash flow can sustain the current dividend, investors should not anticipate aggressive increases in the near future due to these challenges and the company's focus on share buybacks.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Labcorp (NYSE:LH) EVP Sells 87 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Labcorp EVP Jonathan Meltzer sold 87 shares of the company's stock on February 12th for a total value of $25,266.54, reducing his stake by 3.03%. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.72 per share, yielding approximately 1.0%. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Labcorp with an average target price of $300.75.

feed.title: TFS Financial (NASDAQ:TFSL) CEO Marc Stefanski Sells 7,149 Shares
feed.summary: TFS Financial CEO Marc Stefanski sold 7,149 shares of company stock for approximately $107,449, reducing his holdings by 6.11%. This transaction follows the company missing its fourth-quarter earnings and revenue estimates. Despite a 7.6% dividend yield, the company faces a very high dividend payout ratio, and analysts generally maintain a "Hold" rating on the stock.

feed.title: Varonis Systems, Inc. (VRNS) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz announced that investors who suffered losses in Varonis Systems, Inc. (VRNS) have the opportunity to lead a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Varonis failed to disclose that it was ill-equipped to maintain its ARR growth trajectory without a high rate of quarterly conversions, making positive statements about the company's prospects materially misleading. Investors have until March 9, 2026, to participate as lead plaintiff in the action.

feed.title: Americas Silver (TSE:USA) Director Sells C$1,370,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Americas Silver director Scott Mckee sold 125,000 shares of the company's stock for C$1,370,000.00, reducing his holding by 17.4%. Despite the sale and negative financial fundamentals like a negative net margin and return on equity, analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating with a consensus price target of C$8.25. The stock is currently trading above both its 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

feed.title: When COC Meets HDPE: How Colgate Pushed a Familiar Resin Into New Territory
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive, in collaboration with Polyplastics, has successfully developed a method to significantly lightweight HDPE bottles by 30% while maintaining performance and recyclability. This was achieved by blending high-Tg Cyclic Olefin Copolymer (COC) with HDPE, enabling its use in Injection Stretch Blow Molding (ISBM) where it was previously difficult to process. The innovation also results in an opaque bottle without the need for added pigments, which can benefit recycling efforts.

feed.title: Mariner Partners with State Street to Use Its Charles River Wealth Management Platform
feed.summary: Mariner, a U.S. private equity-backed financial services firm, has partnered with State Street to implement its Charles River wealth management solution. This collaboration aims to optimize Mariner's operations, enhance advisor workflows, and support its long-term growth strategy, including a target of reaching 5,000 advisors. The centralized platform is designed to improve efficiency, ensure consistency, and allow advisors to focus more on client relationships.

feed.title: Fund Opens $7.8 Million Position in Farmer Mac Despite 20% S&P Underperformance
feed.summary: First Sabrepoint Capital Management LP recently acquired 44,500 shares of Federal Agricultural Mortgage Corporation (Farmer Mac) for $7.81 million, establishing a new position that represents 3.01% of its reportable assets. This investment comes despite Farmer Mac's stock underperforming the S&P 500 by over 20 percentage points in the past year. However, Farmer Mac's underlying business shows strong growth with increasing business volume, net interest income, and core earnings, suggesting potential unrecognized value for investors.

feed.title: TFS Financial CEO Stefanski sells $107,554 in shares
feed.summary: Marc A. Stefanski, CEO of TFS Financial CORP, sold 7,156 shares of company stock on February 11 and 12, 2026, for a total of approximately $107,554. These sales occurred while the stock was trading near its 52-week high. Despite the sales, Stefanski retains significant direct and indirect holdings, and the company maintains a substantial 7.62% dividend yield, although it recently missed first-quarter earnings and revenue expectations.

feed.title: Xcel Energy (XEL) Upgraded to Buy by UBS with $89 Price Target
feed.summary: UBS has upgraded Xcel Energy (XEL) to a Buy rating with an $89 price target, citing the company's strong 9% EPS growth rate and significant potential for data center load growth. This upgrade led to a 2.9% increase in XEL's share price. Xcel Energy, a major utility provider and one of the largest renewable energy suppliers in the U.S., also expects the Smokehouse fire liability to be under $100 million.

feed.title: Key facts: DexCom reports $2.8B profit; Wells Fargo cuts price target
feed.summary: DexCom, Inc. reported a gross profit of $2.8 billion for 2024, a 15% increase from the previous year, despite a dip in gross profit margin due to supply chain issues. As a result, Wells Fargo and Canaccord Genuity have both lowered their price targets for DXCM. DexCom is also expanding its product line with Smart Basal and anticipates increased insurance coverage for type 2 diabetes, which could broaden its market.

feed.title: Key facts: Delta Air Lines to add A321neo aircraft; $1.3B profit sharing announced
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines will introduce seven A321neo aircraft in May, equipped with 44 Delta First seats and future plans for flatbed suites. The company also announced a $1.3 billion profit-sharing distribution to its employees for the year 2025.

feed.title: Key facts: AIG Files Form 10-K; Wells Fargo Raises Price Target to $87
feed.summary: American International Group (AIG) has filed its Form 10-K with Euronext Dublin, detailing its financial and operational performance for the past year. Concurrently, Wells Fargo has adjusted its price target for AIG shares, increasing it from $85.00 to $87.00.

feed.title: Xcel Energy upgraded to buy, Edison International cut at UBS
feed.summary: UBS has upgraded Xcel Energy to a "buy" rating while simultaneously cutting Edison International to a "neutral" rating. This news reflects a shift in analyst perspective on the investment potential of these two utility companies.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries outlines $1.75–$1.85 Q1 2026 EPS guidance as company focuses on price actions and cost controls
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries has provided its earnings per share guidance for the first quarter of 2026, estimating it to be between $1.75 and $1.85. The company's strategy to achieve these results hinges on implementing price actions and maintaining tight cost controls amidst market conditions. This outlook reflects Mohawk's proactive approach to financial management in the upcoming quarter.

feed.title: Looking Into Molina Healthcare Inc's Recent Short Interest
feed.summary: Molina Healthcare Inc.'s short interest as a percentage of float has decreased by 5.08% since its last report, now standing at 2.89 million shares sold short, which is 7.48% of available regular shares. This reduction in short interest suggests a potentially more bullish sentiment among investors despite the company still having more short interest than its peer group average of 4.78%. It would take traders an estimated 1.96 days to cover their short positions.

feed.title: She guides 800 families; Forbes names her a top women wealth advisor
feed.summary: Lisa Detanna, Managing Director and Private Wealth Advisor at Raymond James, has been recognized on Forbes' 2026 list of America’s Top Women Wealth Advisors. Her practice, Global Wealth Solutions Group, oversees wealth strategies for approximately 800 families, managing $4.4 billion in custodied assets. This honor highlights her 30 years of experience, leadership, and commitment to client education and multi-generational financial planning.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for PPG Indus
feed.summary: This article provides insights into PPG Industries Inc.'s (NYSE: PPG) stock performance and its Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio. As of the current market session, PPG's stock price experienced a slight drop, but saw significant increases over the past month and year. The article notes that PPG's P/E ratio is lower than the Chemicals industry aggregate, suggesting potential undervaluation but also emphasizing the need to consider other financial metrics and industry trends when evaluating investment decisions.

feed.title: Are U.S. small-caps finally back?
feed.summary: U.S. small-cap stocks have shown significant outperformance recently, reversing a long period of underperformance against large-caps. This rebound is attributed to improving fundamentals, including accelerated earnings growth driven by lower interest rates, reduced trade uncertainty, better regional banking conditions, broadening AI benefits, and fiscal stimulus. T. Rowe Price's Asset Allocation Committee believes this rally is durable and has increased its overweight position in U.S. small-cap stocks.

feed.title: PMI Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Picard Medical, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Picard Medical, Inc. (PMI) for alleged securities fraud, covering investors who purchased securities between September 2, 2025, and October 31, 2025. The lawsuit claims Picard engaged in a stock manipulation scheme to inflate its share price, allowing insiders to sell shares at artificially high values. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm by April 3, 2026, to participate in the lawsuit and potentially recover damages.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Howard G. Smith announced an opportunity for F5, Inc. (FFIV) shareholders who incurred losses between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, to lead a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that F5 failed to disclose a significant security incident during this period, leading to materially misleading statements about its business operations and prospects. The deadline for investors to participate as a lead plaintiff is February 17, 2026, and those interested are encouraged to contact the law firm for more information.

feed.title: Toronto-Dominion Bank (TSE:TD) Downgraded by CIBC World Markets to Neutral
feed.summary: CIBC World Markets has downgraded Toronto-Dominion Bank (TSE:TD) from "outperform" to "neutral" but raised its price target to C$136.00, suggesting a potential upside of 5.21%. Despite the downgrade, other analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of C$131.15. The bank reported solid fundamentals, including C$2.18 EPS for the quarter, and traded down C$0.92 at C$129.26 with a market cap of C$216.7 billion.

feed.title: 3 Cheap Old Economy Stocks to Buy Now
feed.summary: This article from Zacks Investment Research identifies three "old economy" stocks with strong value and growth potential: Barclays PLC (BCS), Korea Electric Power Corp. (KEP), and Deutsche Bank (DB). Using a stock screen that looks for a PEG ratio under 1.0 and high Zacks Ranks, the analyst highlights these companies for their cheap valuations (low P/B or P/E ratios) and expected earnings growth. The article suggests these established companies offer attractive investment opportunities amidst a tech stock sell-off.

feed.title: Himax Technologies (NASDAQ:HIMX) Announces Earnings Results, Hits Expectations
feed.summary: Himax Technologies (NASDAQ:HIMX) announced its Q4 earnings, meeting the consensus EPS estimate of $0.04, with revenues slightly exceeding expectations at $203.08 million despite a 14.4% year-over-year decrease. Following the report, the stock experienced a decline on heavy trading volume. The company provided a Q1 2026 EPS guidance of $0.020-$0.040, maintains a "Hold" rating from analysts with an $8.00 target, and institutional investors own a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: Zacks.com featured highlights include AngloGold Ashanti, Columbia Sportswear, Roku and TTM Technologies
feed.summary: Zacks.com highlights four top-ranked liquid stocks—AngloGold Ashanti, Columbia Sportswear, Roku, and TTM Technologies—that investors might consider adding to their portfolios for potential returns. The article explains how liquidity ratios like current, quick, and cash ratios can help identify financially sound companies. Each company's recent performance, growth prospects, and financial estimates are discussed.

feed.title: With 34% Ownership of the Shares, Joby Aviation, Inc. (NYSE:JOBY) Is Heavily Dominated by Institutional Owners
feed.summary: Joby Aviation, Inc. (NYSE:JOBY) is significantly influenced by institutional investors who own 34% of the company's shares, making the stock price sensitive to their trading activities. The top 10 shareholders collectively own more than half of the company, with Toyota Motor Corporation being the largest single shareholder. Insiders also hold a substantial stake of US$1.6 billion, indicating alignment with shareholder interests despite recent insider selling.

feed.title: Public Storage: PS 4.0 launches with new leadership, digital focus, and strong liquidity despite modest 2026 outlook
feed.summary: Public Storage has launched "PS 4.0," a new initiative marked by leadership changes, a digital transformation focus, and the integration of AI. Despite a projected slight decline in 2026 Core FFO per share compared to 2025's $16.97, the company anticipates strong non-same-store growth. The report also highlights Public Storage's robust liquidity and strong balance sheet.

feed.summary: This article identifies that the provided content is an empty MSN article and thus cannot be summarized or processed for its dividend information. The original content is missing.
